title: James Lankford Should Revoke His Endorsement of Larry Boggs
author: James Silberman
orientation: Free the States calls on U.S. Senator James Lankford to rescind his endorsement of Oklahoma State Senator Larry Boggs, arguing that Boggs' record of blocking the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act contradicts Lankford's praise of him as a constitutional and biblical conservative. The piece contrasts Boggs with abolitionist primary challenger Warren Hamilton and frames the endorsement as enabling submission to Roe v. Wade.
audiences: Oklahoma voter weighing the SD7 primary between Boggs and Hamilton
Pro-life Christian troubled by political endorsements that ignore policy records
Abolitionist activist tracking which incumbents block the AAOA
James Lankford supporter or staffer reviewing the endorsement
Journalist or political analyst covering Oklahoma Republican primaries

questions.question: What event prompted Free the States to publicly call on Senator Lankford to revoke his endorsement?
questions.audience: Journalist or political analyst covering Oklahoma Republican primaries
questions.answer: On May 24, 2020, a video of U.S. Senator James Lankford endorsing State Senator Larry Boggs was posted to Boggs' campaign Facebook page, in which Lankford described Boggs as a principled conservative warrior. Free the States published this article five days later arguing the endorsement is incompatible with Boggs' actual legislative record on abortion.
questions.quote: On May 24th, a video featuring U.S. Senator James Lankford endorsing Oklahoma State Senator Larry Boggs was posted to Boggs’ campaign Facebook page.

questions.question: How did Lankford characterize Boggs in the endorsement video?
questions.audience: James Lankford supporter or staffer reviewing the endorsement
questions.answer: Lankford praised Boggs as a man of integrity and wisdom, an old-school freedom-fighter, and a consummate warrior who has spent his life defending the Constitution and biblical principles, including protecting life from preborn to natural death.
questions.quote: “He’s a consummate warrior who fought his whole life to defend the Constitution and Biblical principles.”

questions.question: What specific legislative action did Boggs take that Free the States says contradicts Lankford's praise?
questions.audience: Abolitionist activist tracking which incumbents block the AAOA
questions.answer: On March 11, 2020, Boggs abstained from the vote to place the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act on General Order, effectively withholding the support needed to advance a bill that would have ended abortion in the state.
questions.quote: Boggs chose not to support that bill, abstaining from the vote to place the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act on General Order on March 11.

questions.question: How has Boggs publicly characterized abolitionist organizations?
questions.audience: Oklahoma voter weighing the SD7 primary between Boggs and Hamilton
questions.answer: Boggs has labeled abolitionist organizations as 'wolves in sheep's clothing' on Facebook and his campaign has organized events specifically aimed at criticizing the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act, despite repeated requests from constituents who support abolition.
questions.quote: He has called abolitionist organizations “wolves in sheep’s clothing” on Facebook.

questions.question: Why does the article argue that 'pro-life' submission to the Supreme Court is unprincipled?
questions.audience: Pro-life Christian troubled by political endorsements that ignore policy records
questions.answer: The article contends that politicians like Boggs treat God's word and the Constitution as binding only when the Supreme Court permits, which inverts the meaning of standing on biblical principles—if the Court demands sin (allowing abortion), they comply rather than resist.
questions.quote: If the supreme court tells the State of Oklahoma that we have to sin by allowing the mass murder of preborn children, then sin we must, according to Boggs and many other pro-life politicians.

questions.question: How does the article contrast Warren Hamilton with Larry Boggs?
questions.audience: Oklahoma voter weighing the SD7 primary between Boggs and Hamilton
questions.answer: Boggs is described as accepting submission to Roe v. Wade and the regulatory pro-life model, while Hamilton has pledged to support the immediate abolition of abortion and to reject Roe v. Wade as an unconstitutional opinion that should not be obeyed.
questions.quote: In Hamilton, we’re getting someone who will treat the issue of abortion with the urgency and gravity that it rightly demands; someone who **supports rejecting *Roe v. Wade* as the evil, unconstitutional opinion that it is and the abolition of abortion.**

questions.question: What ultimate moral consequence does the article warn Lankford about if he refuses to revoke the endorsement?
questions.audience: Pro-life Christian troubled by political endorsements that ignore policy records
questions.answer: Beyond predicting political loss in Oklahoma, the article warns that Lankford will be culpable as a delayer of abolition and will have to give an account before God for endorsing a politician who blocks legislation to end abortion.
questions.quote: More importantly, if he does not change course, he will have to answer to the Supreme Judge for this endorsement.

questions.question: What concrete action does the article direct readers to take in response?
questions.audience: Abolitionist activist tracking which incumbents block the AAOA
questions.answer: Readers are given Senator Lankford's office phone number and a link to his contact form so they can directly pressure him to withdraw the endorsement.
questions.quote: U.S. Senator James Lankford’s contact info:

related.title: Disgraceful! Pro-Life Establishment Lies About Abolitionist Candidate Warren Hamilton
related.reason: Documents the establishment counter-attack on Hamilton, the abolitionist challenger framed as the alternative to Boggs in this article.

related.title: Larry Boggs Campaigns With 2019 & 2020 Abortion Rights Men of the Year
related.reason: Provides additional evidence of Boggs' alliances that contradict Lankford's portrayal of him as a pro-life warrior.

related.title: Senator Larry Boggs Supported by Pro-Abortion Democrat Sen. Mary Boren
related.reason: Reinforces the article's case that Boggs' actual political support base is incompatible with Lankford's pro-life endorsement.

related.title: Oklahoma Baptists for Abolishing Abortion Endorses Warren Hamilton for SD7
related.reason: Shows the counter-endorsement landscape Lankford was operating in, with abolitionist Christians backing Hamilton over Boggs.

related.title: Free Will Baptist Abolitionist Leaders and Pastors Endorse Warren Hamilton
related.reason: Another endorsement story illustrating the religious-political coalition opposing the Lankford-backed incumbent.

related.title: Warren Hamilton Defeats Larry Boggs, Margie Alfonso Defeats Clay Iiams
related.reason: Reports the primary outcome that vindicated this article's prediction that Lankford's endorsement would not save Boggs.

related.title: Responding to Larry Boggs' Explanation of Tuesday's Confrontation
related.reason: Continues the running coverage of Boggs' hostile posture toward abolitionists referenced in this article.
